top_10.name: Senso-ji Temple
top_10.description: Tokyo's oldest and most iconic Buddhist temple, featuring the dramatic Kaminarimon Gate and the bustling Nakamise-dori shopping street.

top_10.name: Meiji Jingu Shrine
top_10.description: A grand, serene Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji, nestled inside a massive 170-acre forested park in the middle of the city.

top_10.name: Shibuya Sky
top_10.description: An open-air observation deck sitting 229 meters above Shibuya Crossing, offering breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views of Tokyo.
top_10.booking_requirement: Book in advance (Strictly required for sunset slots)

top_10.name: teamLab Planets
top_10.description: An immersive, multi-sensory digital art museum where visitors walk barefoot through water and massive projection-mapped installations.

top_10.name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
top_10.description: One of Tokyo's largest and most beautiful parks, blending traditional Japanese, English Landscape, and French Formal garden styles.

top_10.name: Tokyo Skytree
top_10.description: The tallest structure in Japan, offering unmatched views spanning all the way to Mount Fuji on a clear day.

top_10.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
top_10.description: A vibrant historic district packed with narrow alleyways hosting hundreds of stalls serving fresh sushi, street food, and kitchenware.

top_10.name: Akihabara Electric Town
top_10.description: The global epicenter of anime, gaming, manga, and electronics subculture, flashing with neon signs and multi-story arcades.

top_10.name: Harajuku & Takeshita Street
top_10.description: The birth place of Japan's extreme kawaii youth fashion, lined with quirky boutiques, crepe stands, and vintage clothing stores.

top_10.name: Ghibli Museum
top_10.description: The whimsical, strictly high-demand museum designed by Hayao Miyazaki, showcasing the animation craft of Studio Ghibli.
top_10.booking_requirement: Book in advance (Required on the 10th of the previous month)

hidden_gems.name: Todoroki Valley
hidden_gems.description: A forested, deep ravine sanctuary hidden completely inside Tokyo's urban sprawl, featuring walking paths, a wooden bridge, and a hidden temple.

hidden_gems.name: Gotokuji Temple
hidden_gems.description: A charming Buddhist temple famous for being the legendary birthplace of the 'Maneki-neko' (beckoning lucky cat), filled with thousands of white cat figurines.

hidden_gems.name: Nezu Shrine
hidden_gems.description: One of Japan's oldest shrines, boasting a striking hillside path lined with thousands of miniature vermillion torii gates and an expansive azalea garden.

top_10.name: Hakone Open-Air Museum
top_10.description: A magnificent outdoor sculpture park harmonized with the surrounding mountains, featuring a dedicated Picasso Pavilion.

top_10.name: Lake Ashi Sightseeing Cruise
top_10.description: A scenic cruise across a volcanic crater lake aboard stylized pirate ships, offering iconic postcard shots of Mt. Fuji.

top_10.name: Owakudani Valley
top_10.description: An active volcanic valley with sulfurous vents and hot springs, famous for its 'Kuro-tamago' (black eggs boiled in the spring water).

hidden_gems.name: Hakone Venetian Glass Museum (Glass Forest)
hidden_gems.description: A whimsical museum complex featuring crystal glass trees and outdoor installations mirroring classical Italian villas in the mountain fog.

hidden_gems.name: Choanji Temple
hidden_gems.description: A serene, quiet forest temple nestled in Sengokuhara, famous for hundreds of distinct stone 'Rakan' statues scattered among mossy trails.

top_10.name: Fushimi Inari Taisha
top_10.description: The legendary mountain shrine featuring paths lined with over 10,000 vibrant vermillion torii gates winding through deep forest.

hidden_gems.name: Gio-ji Temple
hidden_gems.description: A secluded, tiny Zen temple known for its otherworldly emerald moss garden, towering bamboo screen, and a quiet circular viewing window.

hidden_gems.name: Otagi Nenbutsu-ji Temple
hidden_gems.description: A whimsical hidden hillside temple containing exactly 1,200 unique whimsical stone statues, each depicting an expressive, playful human facial trait.

top_10.name: Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park & Museum
top_10.description: A deeply moving, world-class park and memorial complex documenting the atomic bombing, dedicated to global peace.

hidden_gems.name: Mitaki-dera Temple
hidden_gems.description: A highly atmospheric, mossy mountain temple nestled in a steep ravine, famous for historic waterfalls, dynamic stone statues, and a beautiful red pagoda.

top_10.name: Dotonbori District
top_10.description: Osaka's flashy, electric nightlife artery, packed with towering neon billboards and massive animated food signs.

hidden_gems.name: Hozenji Yokocho Alley
hidden_gems.description: A narrow, stone-paved old-world lane hidden just steps from modern Dotonbori, centered around a moss-covered Buddhist statue.
